rationale: Major frictions: the archetype is a young male dancing in a dim blue/dark-toned bedroom referencing a different game (Crossword Go), which clashes hard with Marble Sort's female-skewing 25-55 audience and the soft pastel purple/orange satisfying palette. Live-action UGC dance hooks also fail to communicate the sorting/timing/physics core loop — sorting puzzle UA almost always needs gameplay-forward visuals to convert. Skip this archetype; if testing live-action UGC at all, recast with a 30-45 female creator showing satisfying ASMR-style gameplay reactions instead.
